item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liter hobbles forwards. It is clear that he has sold his toes to Dominican Joe.

Well, this is just the greatest line on this fucking website. +1, 10/10 work here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Face insults get the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite the funny read for something that ultimately sounds like an R-rated, SCP-Themed Jumanji movie in the making.

item.title: I'm Pretty Sure I've Played D&D With Yancy Wondertainment
item.content:encoded: This was absolutely hilarious—the Dreamer in particular got me chuckling as she reminded me of the riddle games I used to play on the schoolbus in third grade (I pictured her looking like a Khajiit, is that about right?). Personally, I didn't mind the Wondertainment infodump at the beginning, but I thought that there were a few too many reminders that the maze was "fucking stupid". I haven't gone back to count how many times its lack of quality was brought up, but for me it crossed the line from "perfectly understandable in-character grumbling" to "telling after showing".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a pretty nice and light-hearted scp/tale, with what I would consider a second-hand genre change considering how this is in the same universe as "to never again see the light of day". +1

(Also a question out of curiosity, from what spanish-speaking country are Sr. Honesto y Sr. Deshonesto?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are one of those scp articles that are so nice you wish you could upvote them twice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about you, but I feel like this is actually a pretty fun maze. Yancy deserves a bit more credit than he gets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ok

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yancy Wondertainment: Excuse you. I'll not have you referring to me by my slave name.

Come on now.

Rest of it is fine for what it is I guess (I generally prefer funny articles to be shorter), can't +1 it in good conscience with something this in it though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Vice: Alright, now hear me out-

Franko: No.

Vice: (Quickly) Cool, cool.

Fuck's sake, Kaktus.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This almost feels like a J article in a good way. Friggin hilarious with an interesting concept. I also love The Face's father complex.

+1

item.title: Re: An SCP about a bad maze.
item.content:encoded:
Note that the images have been sourced, and are the following:

  • header.png (CC BY 2.0)
  • interior.png (CC BY 4.0)
  • face.jpg (CC BY-SA 3.0)
  • cabinet.jpg (CC BY-SA 3.0)

All of these images are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't into the lore aspect of the article WRT Wondertainment, but the sheer strength of the writing and humor outweighs the negatives here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, what a funny article.

I can't exactly fault the Wondertainment employee for spilling the beans and ruining the magic here. Sure — I don't think his conduct is fully justified in-universe with the meta-awareness of the situation being used to explain things (the "that just sounds like exposition" line). But I can appreciate the events surrounding the exchange.

Things I like:

within this collapsible i shall reveal the things i like
yes, good
  • How engaging this entire thing is! Like, holy shit. I haven't read SCP-3301 (although I really want to, now, after this), but I do know I want to see way more of this kind of thing.
  • The connection to other articles. This has a thematic connection to skips like SCP-4444 and SCP-4498 in that it's completely off-the-wall hilarious. It also has an interesting literal connection to SCP-3333 (the door-portal to the 3333 mountain) and your DoA Tale. It makes things feel like they have a spot in a larger universe, y'know?
  • Dominican. Fucking. Joe. He's like a cross between dado and Korg from Ragnarok. I want to see more of him specifically!

Easy +1, this is a great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazing read all the way through dude. I kept getting sucked into the world you built.
+1 especially for all the personality of your characters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed it thoroughly. I was a bit deterred by beginning (Description and what not). Seemed like just your average SCP, nothing special, but when I got to the logs… All I can say now is +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Es un hombre deshonesto que sale de la casa sin nada entre sus genitales y el aire libre, sino una delgada capa de tela.

That part alone fucking killed me, holy shit man. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am really not a fan of the "explaining all of the inner workings of Dr. Wondertainment to the Foundation" thing. I'm fine with it in the Adventures in Capitalism tales, because that's an inside view, but the Foundation knowing what Dr. Wondertainment is and how he operates just totally ruins a lot of what Dr. Wondertainment has going for him. Also it didn't help that I saw how wacky the article was going to be and got bored with it - there's gotta be a good balance of wacky and serious in a non-joke SCP, and this goes way over that line for me in a way that (for example) Bush v. Gore didn't. That plus the over-explanation of Wondertainment pushed this from no-vote to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey boys, wanna go to the dungeon for the afternoon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a serious contender for funniest SCP I've ever read. Easily Top 3. Don't have much more to say.

Liter hobbles forwards. It is clear that he has sold his toes to Dominican Joe.

Could it be that I've beheld the greatest line of writing on the entire site on this day, April 26th 2019? It very well could be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thoroughly enjoyed this. All the different elements taken from different worlds and universes (including the funny nod to SCP-3333) create a surprisingly cohesive whole with enough Wondertainment references and lore, while still keeping it fresh with the face and Yancy. +1 (Don't forget those image credits this time like with SCP-4840).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the maze 2: electric boogaloo

i really liked it, +1

item.title: An SCP about a bad maze.
item.content:encoded:
No real explanation for this - sometimes you just get an idea in your head and need to let that baby bird fly. I hope you enjoy it!

Image credits to follow.
